methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Thea Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Brian Berzin that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader nuclear energy peer set of 11 companies—three public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private entities including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo—though a sufficiently narrow same-stage comparable set was unavailable for robust median benchmarking.

Stage-progression methodology was the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a valuation proxy and applying a standard Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $40M. This represents the primary quantitative anchor given the sparse peer cohort at identical stage and valuation tier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $34M to $46M, with a median point of $40M. This range reflects reasonable upside and downside variance around the stage-progression result but is materially constrained by reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include the absence of true valuation multiples from comparable Series A nuclear companies and the lack of granular financial performance data (revenue, burn rate, capital efficiency) for Thea Energy itself. Any subsequent valuation would benefit substantially from either a larger peer dataset at Series A stage in advanced nuclear, or internal metrics that allow revenue or burn-multiple approaches to be evaluated.
